Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated NT-proBNP, suggesting possible heart failure or significant cardiac stress. There is erythrocytosis (high hemoglobin/hematocrit), dyslipidemia (high LDL, low HDL), mildly elevated alkaline phosphatase, low serum protein, and low serum iron. No evidence of acute myocardial injury (normal troponin). Recommend clinical correlation for cardiac function, further evaluation of erythrocytosis, iron deficiency, and liver/bone status.
Provider Organization: St Matthews Health & Community Centre
